mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising payroll employment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units starts and a 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for residential construction and goods and services, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts and real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
